name: workflow-designer-2
description: Meta-workflow that turns a natural language idea into a complete Mediforce WorkflowDefinition. Enhanced with visual presentations — agents produce HTML views alongside their output so reviewers see flow diagrams and proposal comparisons instead of raw JSON.
preamble:
## Domain Context
This workflow generates structured workflow definitions for the Mediforce platform. All clinical and pharma terminology in workflow descriptions refers to domain modeling constructs.

## Presentation Layer
Agent steps in this workflow produce both result.json (structured data) and presentation.html (visual HTML view). The presentation is shown to reviewers in a sandboxed iframe with Tailwind CSS available. The HTML can access the result data via window.__data__.

steps.name: Describe Workflow Idea
steps.description: Describe the workflow you want to create in natural language. Include the goal, the steps involved, any review/approval loops, and triggers (manual, scheduled, webhook).

params.name: idea
params.description: Natural language description of the workflow to design

params.name: workflowName
params.description: Machine-friendly name for the workflow (kebab-case, e.g. invoice-review)

steps.name: Generate Steps
steps.description: Generate workflow structure (steps, transitions, triggers) from the natural language description. Outputs YAML with step definitions, routing logic, and review loops — plus an HTML flow diagram for visual review.

steps.name: Review Steps
steps.description: Review the generated workflow structure. The visual flow diagram shows steps, transitions, and review loops. Approve if the design matches your intent. Revise to regenerate with feedback.

steps.name: Describe Execution Preferences
steps.description: Provide guidance for how steps should be executed. For example: which steps should always be human, preferred AI provider (Anthropic, DeepSeek), budget constraints, autonomy level, or any other execution preferences the agent should consider.

params.name: preferences
params.description: Execution preferences and constraints (optional — agent will propose defaults if empty)

steps.name: Generate Execution Proposals
steps.description: Generate 1-N execution proposals for the approved workflow structure. Each proposal assigns executor type, agent config, autonomy level, and plugin to every step. Proposals are presented as visual comparison cards with automation breakdowns.

steps.name: Review Execution Proposals
steps.description: Review the proposed execution configurations. The visual comparison shows automation levels, executor assignments, and trade-offs for each proposal. Select the best fit, or revise to regenerate with different preferences.

steps.name: Validate WorkflowDefinition
steps.description: Combine the approved steps with the selected execution config into a complete WorkflowDefinition and validate it against the WorkflowDefinitionSchema. Returns structured errors if invalid.

steps.name: Register WorkflowDefinition
steps.description: Register the validated WorkflowDefinition in the Mediforce platform, making it available for starting new workflow runs.
